title: Pulumi tokens should not be disclosed
description: In most cases, trust boundaries are violated when a secret is exposed in a source code repository or an uncontrolled deployment environment. Unintended people who don’t need to know the secret might get access to it. They might then be able to use it to gain unwanted access to associated services or resources.
recommendation: Review and fix according to the rule guidelines.

checks.message: Pulumi Access Token detected. Pulumi is an infrastructure as code platform for provisioning and managing cloud resources. Access tokens provide full programmatic access to your Pulumi organization, including creating/destroying infrastructure stacks, modifying state files, managing secrets, accessing deployment history, and controlling cloud resources across AWS, Azure, GCP, and other providers. Exposing these tokens enables attackers to deploy unauthorized infrastructure, modify or destroy existing resources, access encrypted secrets, manipulate state files leading to infrastructure drift, and potentially compromise your entire cloud environment. Revoke this token immediately from app.pulumi.com, audit all stack changes and deployments, review access logs for unauthorized activity, rotate all secrets stored in affected stacks, and implement strict access controls with least-privilege principles.

Pulumi Access Token detected in variable assignment. Pulumi is an infrastructure as code platform for cloud resource management. This access token grants complete control over your Pulumi organization including stack management, state manipulation, secret access, and infrastructure deployment across all cloud providers. Exposing this token allows attackers to create, modify, or destroy cloud infrastructure, access sensitive configuration data, manipulate state files, steal encrypted secrets, and potentially compromise your entire cloud environment leading to data breaches, service disruptions, and significant financial impact. Immediately revoke this token from your Pulumi account settings at app.pulumi.com, audit recent stack operations and deployments for suspicious activity, review all access logs, rotate secrets stored in affected stacks, implement environment-based token management with strict access controls, and use service accounts with minimal required permissions for CI/CD pipelines.
